title: Optimized protocol for quantification of nitrite levels in zebrafish tissue samples
author: Caio Maximino
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.sabeaan
materials: Re-suspend the supernatant by vortexing, if needed.

steps.title: Final volumes are as follows
Read the absorbance of each calibration...
steps.instruction: Read the absorbance of each calibration solution with a spectrophotometer at 543 nm. ATTENTION All analyses should be made with at least three technical replicates Thoroughly rinse the cuvettes with distilled water to remove contaminants before each replicate

steps.title: Data should be entered in two...
steps.instruction: Data should be entered in two columns, 'x' and 'y', representing concentration and response, respectively. Plot data and perform a linear regression: a a R 3.4.4 Xubuntu 16.04 https://www.r-project.org/ if(!require(chemCal)){ install.packages('chemCal') library(chemCal)}m Determine linearity and curve parameters using chemCal for R if(!require(chemCal)){ install.packages('chemCal') library(chemCal) } m <- lm(y ~ x, data = your_data) summary(m) calplot(m) Determine linearity and curve parameters using chemCal for R chemCal for R 0.2.1 Xubuntu 16.04 https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/chemCal/index.html Johannes Ranke (https://orcid.org/0000-0003-4371-6538)

steps.title: Calculate limit of detection using chemCal for R
steps.instruction: Calculate limit of detection using chemCal for R

steps.title: The next step in determining sensitivity...
steps.instruction: The next step in determining sensitivity of the assay is to determine the limit of quantification (LOQ). This can also be done using parameters from the linear model, with the 'loq' function from the package chemCal. Substitute 'your_n' by the number of technical replicates in the your assay. a loq(m, n = your_n) Determine limit of quantification with the chemCal package chemCal for R 0.2.1 Xubuntu 16.04 https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/chemCal/index.html Johannes Ranke (https://orcid.org/0000-0003-4371-6538)

steps.title: Precision refers to the dispersion of...
steps.instruction: Precision refers to the dispersion of measured values around an average value, and its numerical value is determined by the relative standard deviation (RSD): RSD = 100 * s / |x̄|Where:s = the sample standard deviationx̄ = sample mean

steps.title: Intermediate precision, or inter-run precision, defines...
steps.instruction: Intermediate precision, or inter-run precision, defines the precision of a measurement made in the same laboratory made by different analysts and/or different days. We follow ANVISA's recommendations by running three technical replicates for the lowest, intermediate, and highest NaO 2 concentrations in two different days and assessing the RSD for each concentration. RSDs higher than 15% indicate low intermediate precision.

steps.title: using a weighted linear model, with...
steps.instruction: using a weighted linear model, with weights derived from the curve made for the accuracy determination. Using chemCal (where new_data refers to the curve made for accuracy determination) chemCal for R 0.2.1 Xubuntu 16.04 https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/chemCal/index.html Johannes Ranke (https://orcid.org/0000-0003-4371-

steps.title: weights Generate predicted values from weighted...
steps.instruction: weights Generate predicted values from weighted linear model (weights based on the results from the linearity assay) weights <- with(new_data, { yx <- split(y, x) ybar <- sapply(yx, mean) s <- round(sapply(yx, sd), digits =

steps.title: w <- round(1 / (s^2), digits = 3) }) new_data
steps.instruction: w <- round(1 / (s^2), digits = 3) }) new_data.means <- aggregate(y ~ x, new_data, mean) n <- lm(y ~ x, w = weights, data = new_data.means) inverse.predict(new_data.means, 30, ws = your_weight) #your_weight determined in previous step for 30 μM inverse.predict(new_data.means, 15, ws = your_weight) #your_weight determined in previous step for 15 μM inverse.predict(new_data.means, 7.5, ws = your_weight) #your_weight determined in previous step for 7.5 μM inverse.predict(new_data.means, 3.75, ws = your_weight) #your_weight determined in previous step for 3.75 μM inverse.predict(new_data.means, 1.875, ws = your_weight) #your_weight determined in previous step for 1.875 μM inverse.predict(new_data.means, 0.9375, ws = your_weight) #your_weight determined in previous step for 0.9375 μM inverse.predict(new_data.means, 0.46875, ws = your_weight) #your_weight determined in previous step for 0.46875 μM inverse.predict(new_data.means, 0.234375, ws = your_weight) #your_weight determined in previous step for 0.234375 μM Generate predicted values from weighted linear model (weights based on the results from the linearity assay)

steps.title: Use the predicted values x i...
steps.instruction: Use the predicted values x i obtained in the previous step to calculate the accuracy at each level, as: Accuracy = ([xi - x v ] / x v ) * 100

steps.title: Sacrifice animal with ice-cold water (<...
steps.instruction: Sacrifice animal with ice-cold water (< 12 ºC) followed by spinal transection.

steps.title: Carefully dissect the brain (http://dx
steps.instruction: Carefully dissect the brain (http://dx.doi.org/10.3791/1717) and head kidney (http://dx.doi.org/10.3791/2839). http://dx.doi.org/10.3791/1717 http://dx.doi.org/10.3791/2839

steps.title: Homogenize the tissue samples in 500 μL Zebrafish PBS (0
steps.instruction: Homogenize the tissue samples in 500 μL Zebrafish PBS (0.1 M, pH 7.3). Due to the low sensitivity of the assay, tissues need to be homogenized in a pool from 5 animals. Tissues can be kept frozen (- 20 ºC) for up to 6 months. Due to the low sensitivity of the assay, tissues need to be homogenized in a pool from 5 animals. PBS for zebrafish Contributed by users

steps.title: Correlation coefficients higher than 0
steps.instruction: Correlation coefficients higher than 0.98 suggest linearity. Error residuals should also be checked, looking for deviations in linearity, presence of atypical samples, heteroscedasticity, and dependence between errors; a well-adjusted curve should present erros with a uniform distribution, average zero and constant variance, and absence of atypical samples. An F-test comparing fits of residuals with a linear vs. quadratic model can also be used.

steps.title: The first step in determining the...
steps.instruction: The first step in determining the precision is to assess the repeatability of the assay (that is, intra-run precision). Run three technical replicates for the lowest, intermediate, and highest NaO 2 concentrations. Determine intra-run precision by calculating the RSD for each of these concentrations. RSDs higher than 15% indicate low precision.

steps.title: Carefully transfer 250 μL of the sample to the cuvette
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer 250 μL of the sample to the cuvette. Disposable polystyrène cuvettes (1ml) G-Biosciences Catalog #786-009

steps.title: Add 125 μL sulfanilamide solution, vortex,...
steps.instruction: Add 125 μL sulfanilamide solution, vortex, and let it rest for 2 min

steps.title: Nitrite stock solution
steps.instruction: Nitrite stock solution: Weight 0.015 g sodium nitrite (NaNO 2 ) and store in a beaker covered in aluminum foil Dilute in 100 ml distilled water (volume measured in a volumetric flask) Store in an ambar flask, identified and kept under refrigeration (2 - 8 ºC) Sodium nitrite P212121 Distilled Water Contributed by users Nitrite solutions should be kept from light by storing in ambar flasks 1 mL of this solution contains 14.4937 μM nitrite

steps.title: Sulfanilamide stock solution
steps.instruction: Sulfanilamide stock solution: Weight 0.5 g sulfanilamide and store in a beaker covered in aluminum foil Dilute in 100 ml of HCl 20% (volume measured in a volumetric flask) Store in an ambar flask, identified and kept under refrigeration (2 - 8 ºC) Sulfanilamide Contributed by users Chloridric acid 20% Contributed by users HCl is highly corrosive for skin https://www.msdsonline.com/2014/09/10/hydrochloric-acid-hazards-safety-tips/ Sulfanilamide solutions should be kept from light by storing in ambar flasks

steps.title: NED [N-(1-Naphthyl)ethylenediamine] stock solution
steps.instruction: NED [N-(1-Naphthyl)ethylenediamine] stock solution: Weight 0.3 g NED and store in a beaker covered in aluminum foil Dilute in 100 mL HCl 1% (volume measured in a volumetric flask) Store in an ambar flask, identified and kept under refrigeration (2 - 8 ºC) N-(1-Naphthyl)-ethylenediamine dihydrochloride Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #NB0650.SIZE.10g Chloridric acid 1% Contributed by users

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL sulfanilamide, suspend with vortex, and allow the solution to rest for 2 min.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL NED, suspend with vortex, and allow the solution to rest for 10 min.

steps.title: Make serial dilutions of the NaNO...
steps.instruction: Make serial dilutions of the NaNO 2 stock solution, adding volumes to aluminum foil-wrapped test tubes (Table on Step 7, below)

steps.title: Centrifuge samples for 10 min at 14,500 rpm
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples for 10 min at 14,500 rpm. The supernatant is used for the assay. Centrifuge Contributed by users

steps.title: Add 125 μL NED solution, vortex,...
steps.instruction: Add 125 μL NED solution, vortex, and let it rest for 10 min

steps.title: Measure absorbance at the spectrophotometer at 542 nm
steps.instruction: Measure absorbance at the spectrophotometer at 542 nm.

steps.title: Interpolate unknowns (sample nitrite) from the...
steps.instruction: Interpolate unknowns (sample nitrite) from the calibration curvem refers to the values derived from the linear model, and unknown_value to the absorbances measured in the assay inverse.predict(m, unknown_value, alpha = 0.

steps.title: Interpolate unknowns (sample nitrite) from the...
steps.instruction: Interpolate unknowns (sample nitrite) from the calibration curve m refers to the values derived from the linear model, and unknown_value to the absorbances measured in the assay

steps.title: Run a calibration curve by following...
steps.instruction: Run a calibration curve by following the steps for linearity (Steps 4-8)

steps.title: Correct nitrite values by protein levels,...
steps.instruction: Correct nitrite values by protein levels, measured using the Bradford assay.
